hypothesis: If a multimodal model is trained to jointly encode graph, text, and XRD modalities with cross-modal prediction, VIC regularization, and contrastive loss, then it will achieve lower MAE on crystal property prediction (including band gap, shear modulus, bulk modulus, dielectric constant, and perovskite formation energy) and better generalization to AFLOW band gap data than state-of-the-art graph-only baselines (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN) because combining complementary structural, textual, and diffraction information in a shared latent space yields richer, more predictive representations.
